mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.2% as of Oct 2015 indicates sustained accommodation
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, producing positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained job creation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%) when PAYEMS growth averages > 200k/month, or Sharpe ratio < 0.3 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.0 million annualized units combined with rising retail sales indicate strong consumer demand and construction activity that support corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 626k and RSAFS at 366,404M as of Sep 2015
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances when both HOUST > 1000 and RSAFS > 360000, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining below 102.0 signals weakening manufacturing and supply-side momentum that constrains corporate profit growth and earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.27 as of Oct 2015, near threshold
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when INDPRO < 102.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession risk
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances when UNRATE > 5.0%, or the mechanism fails to predict drawdowns during labor market stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL > 220) combined with accommodative federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1.0%) creates financial repression that erodes real returns and forces equity reallocation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.73 and FEDFUNDS at 0.2% as of Sep-Oct 2015
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) expanding at annualized rates above 2.5% signals sustained economic expansion that supports equity valuations and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,254.7B (2017 dollars) as of Q2 2015
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances during periods of GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals tight supply-demand balance and rising wage pressures that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both conditions deteriorate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.27 and UNRATE at 5.1% as of Oct 2015
regime_hint: late-cycle stagflation risk with supply shocks
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 53% of instances when both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously, or the joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
